task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The original image is loaded in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The original image (either a colorful gradient or a construction site photo) is visible on the GIMP canvas.

task_completion.description: The Emboss filter dialog is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Emboss' dialog box is visible on the screen, typically accessed via Filters -> Distorts -> Emboss.

task_completion.description: The emboss effect is previewed on the canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: While the Emboss dialog is open, the image on the canvas shows a grayish, 3D raised relief effect with bumps and hollows.

task_completion.description: The emboss effect is applied to the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Emboss dialog is closed and the final image on the canvas retains the grayish, 3D raised relief effect.

integrity.description: Agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the filter
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks through GIMP menus and dialogs rather than typing image manipulation commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The effect is applied to the provided original image
integrity.visual_evidence: The original colorful gradient or construction site photo is seen before the filter is applied, rather than a pre-embossed image being loaded.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the GUI manually
integrity.visual_evidence: No Python-fu or Script-fu console is used to bypass the standard GUI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses GIMP 3.0. The input image is either a colorful gradient or a construction site photo. The Emboss filter 'stamps and carves the active layer or selection, giving it relief with bumps and hollows'. A correctly applied emboss effect will significantly reduce the image's saturation (making it grayish) and create a 3D relief appearance. Note: The programmatic verifier has a calibration issue where it expects high edge enhancement, but the actual GIMP emboss filter compresses pixel values. The VLM should visually verify the grayish 3D relief effect on the canvas, ignoring any programmatic verifier failures.
